item.title: MP's husband and two men bailed after arrests over alleged China spying
item.description: One of those arrested is the husband of Scottish Labour MP Joani Reid.

item.title: Iranian Drones Fall in Azerbaijan as Conflict Spreads Farther Beyond Mideast
item.description: Azerbaijan says the attack “will not remain unanswered,” as the neighbors with a long history of tensions found themselves again at odds.

item.title: Iran targets headquarters of Iranian Kurdish forces in Iraq
item.description: The Iranian military's strikes come amid speculation that the US wants Iranian Kurdish groups to join its conflict with Iran.

item.title: In maps: Six days of strikes across the Middle East
item.description: Israel has continued strikes across Iran and Lebanon as Donald Trump warns action could continue for weeks

item.title: Iran Calls Torpedoing of Ship by U.S. an ‘Atrocity’
item.description: Iran’s foreign minister accused the United States of committing an “atrocity at sea,” after a U.S. Navy submarine attacked an Iranian frigate in the Indian Ocean.

item.title: Reform leads other parties in donations ahead of May elections
item.description: The latest donations received by Nigel Farage's party includes £3m from crypto investor Christopher Harborne.

item.title: Investigative journalism in India is under threat
item.description: Politicians and tycoons are tightening their grip

item.title: Russia’s Starlink shutdown is a blow to its soldiers and drones
item.description: Launching its own satellite network is not realistic

item.title: Donald Trump must stop soon
item.description: His ill-considered conflict risks descending into chaos

item.title: Track Star’s Jack Coyne leads a musical tour of New York
item.description: The TikTok host takes us to the jazz clubs, concert venues, bars and record stores that keep the city swinging

item.title: Cyprus criticises UK's response to drone attack ahead of Healey visit
item.description: The Cypriot government has criticised how the UK has handled information sharing after drone attacks.

item.title: Who Is Eyal Zamir, the Architect of Israel’s War Plans?
item.description: The Israeli general has long been focused on the threat from Iran and its proxies, and has called for a regional alliance against the country.

item.title: It’s time to unleash Europe’s pensions
item.description: One reform offers both security in old age and dynamism now

item.title: Iran’s Friends Include China and Russia. But Where are They Now?
item.description: Iran maintains ties with a range of countries, including Turkey, India, Russia and China. Yet in this war, their support is mostly rhetoric.

item.title: Trump Wants a Quick Victory in Iran. But the War May Be Costly.
item.description: The opening days of the conflict are challenging the idea that President Trump can project force abroad while safeguarding American lives and the economy.

item.title: South African President on Trump’s Oval Office ‘Ambush’ and ‘Racist’ Policies
item.description: In an interview with The New York Times, President Cyril Ramaphosa opened up about the role of middle powers, relations with Washington and apartheid.

item.title: Asylum seekers who break law to have support removed
item.description: The home secretary will say in a speech the move is required to restore "trust" to the asylum system.

item.title: Senate Republicans Block War Powers Limits as Mideast Crisis Widens
item.description: The Defense Department released the name of a fifth American killed in an Iranian attack on Sunday, and released the name of another soldier believed to have died in the same incident. The State Department said a charter flight was returning to the U.S. with Americans wanting to leave the region.

item.title: Veteran Bihar chief minister to step down for move to parliament
item.description: Nitish Kumar says the state's new government will have his full cooperation and guidance.

item.title: Reform gains second big donation from Thailand-based crypto investor
item.description: Populist party received £3mn from Christopher Harborne in November last year, on top of £9mn in August

item.title: US sub sinks Iranian warship in Indian Ocean, Hegseth says
item.description: Sri Lanka's navy says around 140 people are feared missing after a military vessel went down off its southern coast.

item.title: Why did US and Israel attack Iran and how long could the war last?
item.description: Iran continues retaliatory strikes against Israel and across the Gulf, as US and Israeli attacks on Iran enter a fifth day.

item.title: Global order is 'breaking down', Carney tells Australian parliament
item.description: The Canadian prime minister said Australia and Canada should work together as 'strategic cousins'.

item.title: China sets lowest economic growth target since 1991
item.description: It is also the first time the target has been lowered since it was cut to "around 5%" in 2023.

item.title: The Washington Roundtable on the Iran War
item.description: Is the U.S. repeating the mistakes of the invasion of Iraq?

item.title: Venezuela and US to work together on mining developments, Rodríguez says
item.description: Venezuela's interim president made the announcement after meeting with US interior minister Doug Burgum at the presidential palace in Caracas.

item.title: The devastating conflict where both sides have reasons to keep fighting
item.description: A strong supply of weapons and a history of long wars make Sudan's conflict particularly troubling.

item.title: Dirty laundry and chocolate bars: How Venezuelan prisoners smuggled messages out of jail
item.description: Prisoners recently released from detention in Venezuela describe the conditions under which they were held.

item.title: US Senate vote fails to rein in Trump war powers on Iran
item.description: The measure, passed largely along party lines, would halt US military action until approved by Congress.

item.title: Watch: Moment wolf rescued from canal in northern Italy
item.description: Video released by the Italian fire brigade shows the wolf being rescued from under a bridge.

item.title: Millions of Cubans plunged into darkness as fuel crisis deepens
item.description: Fuel shortages have been exacerbated by a US oil blockade since the seizure of Venezuelan President Nicolás Maduro.

item.title: What to Know About Nepal's Gen Z Election
item.description: Nepal is holding its first election since a youth-led uprising against corruption toppled the government last year. Here is what to know.

item.title: How depleted weapons stockpiles could affect the Iran conflict
item.description: Weapons stocks will not alone decide the outcome of this conflict, but it's certainly a significant factor, writes the BBC's Jonathan Beale.

item.title: In times of chaos, Europe is the muddled power the world needs
item.description: In praise of the dreaded European working group

item.title: Spain's Pedro Sánchez hits back at Trump threat to sever trade saying 'no to war'
item.description: Spain's prime minister delivers a strong rebuttal to US President Donald Trump's threat to end trade with his country.

item.title: Britain Arrests a Lawmaker’s Husband on Suspicion of Spying for China
item.description: Joani Reid, a Labour member of parliament, said she had never “seen anything to suspect” her husband had broken the law. The Metropolitan Police said three men were arrested under the National Security Act.

item.title: U.S. Submarine Torpedoed Iranian Warship Off Sri Lanka as Conflict Widens
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth said it was the first such strike since World War II. The Sri Lankan authorities said they had rescued 32 sailors from the crew of 180.

item.title: Stock markets and oil prices still volatile over fears Iran war may drag on
item.description: Experts have warned that if oil and gas prices remains elevated it could make goods and services more expensive.

item.title: The Iran war has been a stunning aerial success
item.description: Even if, at the political level, its direction is a mess

item.title: Partner of UK MP arrested on suspicion of spying for China, BBC told
item.description: The Met Police suspect three men of assisting Chinese intelligence services in the UK.

item.title: Allen century powers New Zealand to victory over South Africa in T20 World Cup semi-final
item.description: Finn Allen strikes an unbeaten century as New Zealand demolish 2024 runners-up South Africa by nine wickets to reach a second Men's T20 World Cup final

item.title: China needs a more ambitious growth target
item.description: Otherwise a fourth year of deflation awaits

item.title: Russia blames Ukrainian naval drones as tanker sinks in Mediterranean
item.description: The Arctic Metagaz went down between Libya and Malta after it was hit by explosions and a fire, Libyan officials say.

item.title: Iran postpones Khamenei funeral as US-Israeli bombardment continues
item.description: An official says more preparations are needed for the three-day ceremony in Tehran, where the supreme leader will lie in state.

item.title: Starmer defends Iran response as Badenoch calls for more action
item.description: It comes after Trump criticised the PM for refusing to allow the use of UK bases in the initial US-Israel strikes.

item.title: US and Ecuador forces launch operation to fight drug trafficking
item.description: Washington says the two countries are combatting 'designated terrorist organisations' in Ecuador.

item.title: The start of the Iran war was determined by spying success
item.description: But even when assassinations come off, the long-term consequences are unclear

item.title: Nigeria halts Christian pilgrimages to Holy Land over Middle East conflict
item.description: NCPC say the measure is necessary to prioritise the "safety and comfort" of Nigerian pilgrims.

item.title: Ayatollah Ali Khamenei’s Son Emerges as Leading Choice to Be His Successor
item.description: If Mojtaba Khamenei is chosen by Iran’s senior clerics, it may signal a victory for hard-liners, an analyst said.

item.title: Israel tells people in large parts of southern Lebanon to leave ahead of attacks
item.description: Civilians are being displaced, taking shelter in tents, parks and cars, as Israel retaliates to Hezbollah rocket attack.

item.title: Satellite images show Iranian navy and nuclear sites hit by US-Israeli strikes
item.description: US-Israeli strikes have destroyed at least 11 Iranian naval vessels, images reviewed by BBC Verify show.

item.title: With Fuel Running Out and Canadian Flights Suspended, Cuba’s Tourism Is Collapsing
item.description: The Trump administration’s decision to cut off foreign oil to the island is devastating its tourism industry, a key source of income for a government being pushed to the edge.

item.title: Nigerian doctors suspended over death of Adichie's son
item.description: The author's 21-month-old son died in January after complications during medical procedures at a private hospital in Lagos.

item.title: Spain’s Leader, Rejecting Iran War, Escalates Long Feud With Trump
item.description: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez has lashed out at the American-Israeli strikes, underlining his refusal to participate even after President Trump threatened Madrid with economic retaliation.

item.title: Greek court upholds criminal organisation verdict on neo-Nazi Golden Dawn
item.description: The party's founder Nikos Mihaloliakos and other officials are again found guilty, almost six years after an original trial.

item.title: South Africa's long-serving former defence minister and former Robben Island prisoner dies
item.description: Mosiuoa Lekota was imprisoned with former South African President Nelson Mandela on Robben Island.

item.title: Ex-Labour mayor and councillor join Reform UK
item.description: Former Newham councillors Sir Robin Wales and Clive Furness say Labour has failed London.

item.title: Global Leaders Brace for the Fallout From a Fast Metastasizing War
item.description: Higher energy prices, political instability and a potential new wave of refugees: The escalating regional conflict in the Middle East could have far-reaching effects.

item.title: UK to stop study visas from Cameroon and Sudan due to 'abuse'
item.description: The government will end study visas from Afghanistan, Cameroon, Myanmar and Sudan, due to what she says is abuse.

item.title: What to know about the 'two sessions', China's biggest political meeting
item.description: The carefully choreographed annual meetings signal the priorities of the world's second-largest economy.

item.title: After U.S. Attacks on Iran, FIFA Grapples With How to Handle the World Cup
item.description: Soccer’s global governing body is grappling with a World Cup first: a tournament host going to war with a participating nation.

item.title: Lorries skipping border checks is disease risk - MPs
item.description: Government data shows a rise in trucks failing to turn up at Sevington after arrival in Kent.

item.title: Where Are China’s A.I. Doomers?
item.description: Chinese policymakers and the public have expressed high levels of optimism about A.I., even as many in the West worry about the technology’s effects on employment or humanity in general.

item.title: Kathmandu empties as about 800,000 Nepalis head home to vote
item.description: Authorities have reported hours-long jams over the weekend while photos show buses lining highways.

item.title: South Korea woman and doctors guilty of murder of newborn baby
item.description: The case has gripped South Korea, where there is no regulation on how far into pregnancy an abortion can take place.

item.title: Japanese court upholds order to dissolve Unification Church
item.description: The church popularly known as Moonies came under scrutiny after the 2022 killing of ex-PM Shinzo Abe.

item.title: Chris Mason: Trump belittles Starmer with 'no Churchill' jibe but can the special relationship recover?
item.description: The remarks came amid a row over the refusal to permit the use of UK bases for the initial US-Israel strikes.

item.title: U.S. Hunts for Militants as the Nigerian Military Is Accused of Inaction
item.description: The country has one of the most feared armed forces in Africa, yet within its own borders, it has repeatedly failed to stop abductions and attacks.

item.title: Australian girl, 8, killed in snowmobile accident in Japan
item.description: It is the fourth Australian death at a Japanese ski resort this year.

item.title: Ethiopia experiments with 'smart' police stations that have no officers
item.description: A project to introduce unmanned sites is part of a broader adoption of digital technologies.

item.title: 'All red lines have been crossed': Gulf states weigh response to Iranian strikes
item.description: Retaliatory Iranian strikes on Gulf states have tarnished their image as safe and prosperous, writes the BBC's Barbara Plett-Usher.

item.title: US and Israel bomb Iran for fourth day as Trump says air defences 'gone'
item.description: Iran is responding to the strikes by launching missile and drone attacks on Israel and Gulf states hosting US bases.

item.title: UK to send Royal Navy warship HMS Dragon to Cyprus
item.description: The prime minister said the "UK is fully committed to the security of Cyprus and British military personnel based" on RAF Akrotiri.

item.title: As Trump Bashes Spain on Military Spending, German Chancellor Merz Looks On
item.description: Friedrich Merz has called for greater European unity, but he did not publicly object to the president rebuking Spain and Britain during an Oval Office meeting.

item.title: European allies rush to bolster Cyprus defences after drones target British base
item.description: The Cypriot government suspects the Iran-backed group Hezbollah in Lebanon targeted the UK's RAF Akrotiri base in Cyprus.

item.title: Funerals held for students and staff after strike on Iran school
item.description: Iranian authorities say they were killed in a US-Israeli strike in the city of Minab on Saturday.

item.title: Trump says Starmer is 'no Winston Churchill' over Iran strikes
item.description: Sir Keir Starmer had refused to grant the US permission to use the Diego Garcia military base.

item.title: Are Gulf states running out of missile interceptors?
item.description: Their rulers insist not but they are burning through their stockpiles fast

item.title: Trump lashes out at Spain, says he is 'going to cut off all trade'
item.description: The US president criticised the Spanish government after it blocked the US from using its bases during the attack on Iran.

item.title: New charges for son of Norway's crown princess on trial for rape
item.description: Marius Borg Høiby now faces a total of 40 charges at his trial and denies the most serious allegations.

item.title: U.S. Attacks on Iran Test Fragile Truce With China
item.description: Beijing has condemned the U.S.-backed strikes on Iran, a close partner. Yet with trade talks looming, it is unlikely to risk a rupture with Washington.

item.title: In Myanmar, Junta Leader Eyes the Title of President
item.description: The military ruler of Myanmar, Senior Gen. Min Aung Hlaing, is expected to seek a civilian post to add a veneer of legitimacy after sham elections.

item.title: Fears over food shortages in Tehran as residents worry about length of war
item.description: Locals tell BBC Persian they are worried about the availability of groceries and price increases as strikes continue to hit the capital.

item.title: Belgium detains three suspected Cameroon separatists in war crimes probe
item.description: Money is reportedly being raised by them for the armed struggle, prosecutors say.

item.title: What France’s new nuclear-arms doctrine means for Europe
item.description: Emmanuel Macron will build more warheads and include allies

item.title: Reeves says her plan is working as growth forecast cut for this year
item.description: The forecasts were made before the conflict in the Middle East broke out which could have a "very significant" impact, the OBR said.

item.title: Why Europe's leaders have struggled to speak as one on Iran
item.description: European nations say they want to work better together but have differing priorities

item.title: Battered and isolated, Hezbollah drags Lebanon into another war
item.description: Its attack on Israel in support of Iran has led to fresh mass displacements and Israeli bombing, to the fury of many Lebanese.

item.title: Anglican divisions deepen as rebel clerics pick rival to first female leader
item.description: Conservative clerics are meeting in Nigeria to choose a rival to the first female Archbishop of Canterbury.

item.title: Cuba’s economic divides are widening
item.description: Luck and guile increasingly determine who gets how much

item.title: Go further on regulating leasehold managing agents, Rayner urges
item.description: Ex-housing secretary praises the government's draft bill but calls for more action on service charges.

item.title: Mafia boss behind notorious murders in Italy dies behind bars in Milan
item.description: "Nitto" Santapaola was believed to have masterminded the murder of one of Italy's most famous anti-mafia prosecutors in 1992.

item.title: Zelensky fears Trump's Iran war could hurt Ukraine
item.description: Ukraine fears what soaring oil prices and a shortage in air defences could mean for them.

item.title: We still have no idea where this war is heading
item.description: It's hard to predict how the war will end - but those fighting know how they would like it to.

item.title: Mexican drug lord 'El Mencho' buried in golden coffin
item.description: Nemesio Oseguera led the feared Jalisco New Generation Cartel until his death as a result of a firefight in February.

item.title: Why Ali Khamenei may have welcomed the nature of his death
item.description: The ideology of martyrdom could prolong Iran’s regime—and the war

item.title: India's top court angry after junior judge cites fake AI-generated orders
item.description: In several recent instances, AI has disrupted court proceedings in India and elsewhere.

item.title: China removes three retired generals from national advisory body
item.description: Authorities gave no explanation for the ousters but they come as purges of military top brass pick up pace.

item.title: The little town making nearly all of China's lanterns
item.description: A remarkable 80% of the country's lanterns are still made by hand in one small town in Hebei Province.

item.title: Strait of Hormuz: What happens if Iran shuts global oil corridor?
item.description: Iran says it will "set fire" to ships trying to sail through the world's most vital oil transit point.

item.title: Parents of nursery abuse victims to meet minister
item.description: Parents of Vincent Chan's victims will meet the education secretary to press her on safeguarding.

item.title: Can Donald Trump Win a War with Iran If He Can’t Explain Why He Started It?
item.description: So far, explanations are few and the goals—from regime change to ending a nuclear program the President already claimed to have “obliterated”—are many.

item.title: The Iran war is rapidly engulfing the region
item.description: How far could each side escalate?

item.title: What is behind the strategy to take out Iran's leadership?
item.description: Security analyst Gordon Corera looks at what is likely to happen after much of Iran's most senior leadership is killed.

item.title: Hizbullah, the reluctant proxy, strikes at Israel
item.description: But the group’s limited actions suggest that it does not want to be in a war that could finish it off

item.title: India and Canada reset ties with 'landmark' nuclear energy deal
item.description: A host of deals, including one to supply India with uranium, is unveiled as Mark Carney meets Narendra Modi in Delhi.

item.title: Carney to push his middle power strategy during Australia visit
item.description: Defence, trade, and the global place of "middle powers" will be on the agenda as Prime Minister Mark Carney meets his Australian counterpart, Anthony Albanese.

item.title: Can Viktor Orban be beaten?
item.description: We are tracking the polls for Hungary’s election in April

item.title: Jacinda Ardern's move to Australia renews spotlight on New Zealand's brain drain problem
item.description: New Zealand has long struggled to keep its best and brightest with about 180 Kiwis leaving a day.

item.title: Israel strikes Lebanon after Hezbollah rocket fire as Iran conflict widens
item.description: Lebanese officials say 52 people were killed by Israeli strikes, while there are no reports of casualties in Israel.

item.title: The War Room newsletter: A widening war in the Middle East
item.description: Shashank Joshi, our defence editor, asks how long the escalation in Iran can last

item.title: MPs' pay to rise by 5% to £98,599 a year from April
item.description: Westminster's expenses watchdog says MPs are dealing with higher levels of complex casework and abuse.

item.title: France to boost nuclear arsenal and extend deterrence to European allies
item.description: Emmanuel Macron said eight countries could enjoy protection from France's nuclear umbrella - but that Paris would retain sole decision-making power.

item.title: Farage calls for changes to election voting rules
item.description: The Reform UK leader says postal voting should be restricted to those with a 'valid reason'.

item.title: Refugee status becomes temporary in asylum shake-up
item.description: Adults and accompanied children claiming asylum will only receive refugee status for 30 months under the changes.

item.title: Two drones intercepted heading for RAF base, Cyprus says
item.description: No casualties were reported and the base sustained "minimal damage", the Ministry of Defence said.

item.title: Weekend voting to be piloted in May elections
item.description: Findings from the experiment will be published later this year and inform future electoral reform.

item.title: Fifteen years after Fukushima, Japan faces an energy dilemma
item.description: Restarting the world’s largest nuclear plant will not resolve its problems

item.title: China’s ice-cold calculus over Iran
item.description: In the Middle East, it is a political weakling but an economic force
